name: Claude Code 101
description:
**Claude Code 101**

By the end of this workshop you’ll complete **an A.I. powered web application** using a repeatable Claude Code workflow. You'll also have access to direct support for your project and future projects.

**Examples:**
\- personal portfolio\, freelancer website\, small business website
\- website with user input\, backend logic\, stored data\, and public\-data import
\- web app that collects information from forms\, APIs\, spreadsheets\, or public websites—and organize it into a usable database
\- signup funnels\, contact forms\, lead capture pages\, event registration form
\- searchable directory
\- local resource board

You'll accomplish in a group setting with a starter claude code workspace and hands-on technical help.

*First you publish something, then you make it interactive, then you make it intelligent.*

We’ll use a **requirements-driven** flow: define the job, define “done,” lock constraints—and land a **deployment-ready web app** you can actually use and share.

**Cost:** $100

**What you need:**

* A goal that can be described as a website or web application
* A laptop
* Willingness to follow along and run commands (paint-by-number style)
* Optional: a real workflow/problem you want the app to handle (there will be time reserved to run your own projects!)

**Schedule:**

* **6pm - 7pm:** We’ll get everyone running the software stack locally, and the required accounts set up and connected (anthropic, render, github) and get a static website up
* **7pm - 8pm:** we'll do a few tight iterations of the loop (**requirements → plan → build → run → improve**) until you’ve shipped a deployment-ready baseline of your goal. We’ll repeat the same loop across all three exercises so you leave with a pattern you can reuse on your own projects.
* **8pm - 9pm :** We'll continue the above and optionally apply the same template to **your** workflow/problem. I’ll help you adapt the app(s) to your “X” and get you pointed at a clean next step (and optionally deploy if you’re not already live).

**Location**
McCully Library Meeting Area
2211 S King St, Honolulu, HI 9682

name: Fun-Storming Event!
description:
Note: Please register on Luma!
**https://luma.com/m0tu1qe5​**

Join us for an evening of “Fun-storming!” Think of it like brainstorming, but with the pressure dialed way down and the creativity turned way up.
​**How it works:**

* ​**🎤 The Pitch:** You’ll hear from one or two local businesses sharing a real world challenge they are facing.
* ​**💡The Huddle:** We’ll break into random groups to chat through potential solutions.
* ​**⚙️The Mix:** We’ll rotate groups throughout the night so you can meet as many new people as possible.
* ​**🎯 The Goal**: Have Fun!

​**Logistics:**

* ​**🍴Food & Drink:** We’ll provide some light snacks. Please feel free to bring your own food or beverages to share or enjoy.
* ​**✉️RSVP**: [Sign up on Luma](https://luma.com/m0tu1qe5)!

​We can’t wait to see what we can come up with together.

name: Tech Meetup
description:
Low-key in-person meetup for people who are either in tech, interested in starting a career in tech, or love playing with tech as a hobby.

The meeting is at Whole Foods in Kaka'ako, in the outside cafe seating.

More info on the Honolulu Tech Network website:
[https://honolulu.network/~tech](https://honolulu.network/~tech/)

There's usually no real set topic, often people ask what we talk about so here's a sampling of common topics:

* AI
* ChatGPT
* Career stuff
* Other tech communities and events
* 3D printing
* VR/AR
* Web development
* Agile
* What places exist in Honolulu and where they are
* Renewable energy

name: Future Builders: Leading Hawai'i's Tech Renaissance [Hawaii Tech Week]
description:
**We are excited to announce that this event will be running during Hawaii Tech Week .** More info to come. **Date / Time / Location is tentative**. Please see https://hawaiitechweek.com/ . **Mahalo!**

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

Did you know that Hawai'i has a rich youth robotics scene?

When **Hawaiʻi’s robotics teams compete at the world championships**, they’re not just building robots—they’re building the skills our islands need. Join us for an afternoon **celebrating their achievements** and **connecting students, job seekers, career-changers, and local companies** investing in Hawaiʻi’s tech future. With robot demos!

\-\-\-
**Tentative Schedule:**

**10am** *A.I. Hackathon / Workshops / Job mixer (details coming soon)*

**11:30am** *Pupus by Poke for the People*
Musubi, ahi rolls and vegan options from the legendary Poke for the People.

**12:30pm** *Youth Robotics Showcase*
Watch Hawai'i's VEX IQ teams demonstrate their championship robots. See the problem-solving, collaboration, and technical skill that took them from Honolulu garages to competing against 800+ teams worldwide.

**1:30pm** *Panel Discussion*
Q&A Style Panel for youth robotics leaders, tech mentors and robotics entrepreneurs.

**2:15pm** *Mahalos & Goodbyes*
Beach time!

\-\-\-

Our showcase features local robotics startups, teams from [Island Robotics ](https://www.friendsofislandrobotics.org/) and coaches like **Jared Haiola**, who has guided dozens of Hale'iwa students to VEX IQ World Championships over the past decade. His students don't just learn robotics—they learn to solve problems with limited resources, collaborate under pressure, and build things that work.

**Free Admission** *For Families & Individuals*
Bring your kids. Let them see what's possible when Hawai'i's youth get the right tools and mentorship. Let them talk to the teams. Let them imagine themselves building the technology our islands need.

**Become a Sponsor** *Reach out!*
Kevin@Oahu.AI

\-\-\-\-\-
*We will be checking registrations at the door. Keiki are welcome and do not need to be registered for the event. Organizers may refuse entry or remove attendees to maintain a safe environment. Mahalo for understanding.*
\-\-\-\-

*If you share your mobile number on an O'ahu A.I. event form, we may text you about event reminders and updates. Msg frequency varies. Msg and data rates may apply. Reply STOP to opt out. See www.oahu.ai/privacy*
